title: carex dioica subsp. davalliana
intro: Carex dioica subsp. davalliana is a subspecies of the sedgy sedge plant, commonly found in wetland areas and often confused with Carex dioica. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation. The health benefits of Carex dioica subsp. davalliana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or combined with other herbs in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
